skill.name: spark-optimization
skill.description: Guides Apache Spark performance tuning with partitioning, joins, caching, memory settings, shuffle reduction, data formats, and debugging patterns.
skill.summary: Tune Spark jobs for faster, cheaper, and more reliable data processing.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported command execution, network, key, weak-crypto, reconnaissance, and combined-risk patterns. Manual review found these are Markdown code fences, sample Spark snippets, comments, and documentation links, not executable skill behavior. No credential access, malicious network activity, prompt injection, or hidden execution path was found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Code Fences Flagged as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The detected backticks are Markdown inline code or fenced code block delimiters around documentation and PySpark examples. They do not define executable Ruby, shell, or skill runtime behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed locations are Markdown delimiters or inline documentation syntax in SKILL.md. No script file, command runner, or user-controlled command construction exists in the scanned file.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Documentation Links Flagged as Network Access
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URLs are resource links to Spark and Databricks documentation. They are not fetch calls, webhooks, download commands, or automatic network requests.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs appear only in the Resources section as Markdown links. There is no code that transmits data or calls these URLs automatically.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Spark keySet Example Flagged as Certificate Access
low_findings.description: The key-related static hit is a PySpark JVM API example that iterates executor memory status keys. It does not access certificate files, private keys, or secret material.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line calls getExecutorMemoryStatus().keySet() inside monitoring sample code. The surrounding context is memory diagnostics, not file or credential access.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Optimization Text Flagged as Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: The weak-crypto hits are words inside the skill description, Spark explain mode names, and DataFrame sorting examples. No MD5, SHA1, DES, or cryptographic operation is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain ordinary Spark optimization wording and DataFrame examples. No cryptographic library, hash algorithm, or security-sensitive transformation is shown.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Spark Diagnostics Flagged as Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: The reconnaissance hits are comments and Spark monitoring examples for checking partitions, active stages, and broadcast joins. They do not enumerate host files, users, environment variables, or network topology.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding sections describe Spark performance measurement within an application job. No host-level reconnaissance command or external discovery behavior appears.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Combined Dangerous-Pattern Heuristic Dismissed
low_findings.description: The combined code execution, network, and credential-access heuristic is not supported by manual review. The source is a single Markdown guidance file with examples and links, and no executable integration code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Each component signal was traced to benign Markdown or Spark documentation content. No semantic evidence of credential access, exfiltration, or command execution was found.

content.value_statement: Slow Spark jobs waste cluster budget and delay data delivery. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code practical tuning patterns for faster Spark pipelines.
actual_capabilities: Explains Spark execution stages, tasks, shuffles, and partition sizing tradeoffs.
Provides patterns for repartitioning, coalescing, partition pruning, and partitioned writes.
Guides join optimization with broadcast joins, bucketed joins, AQE, and salting for skew.
Covers caching, persistence levels, checkpointing, and memory configuration choices.
Suggests monitoring steps using query plans, Spark UI metrics, and partition skew checks.
Recommends file format settings for Parquet, Delta Lake, compression, and predicate pushdown.
limitations: It provides guidance and examples, not automatic profiling of a live Spark cluster.
It cannot verify cluster-specific settings without job metrics, logs, or configuration details.
The examples use placeholder S3 paths and must be adapted before production use.
It does not replace vendor documentation for version-specific Spark or Databricks behavior.

use_cases.title: Reduce Slow Batch Runtime
use_cases.description: Review a slow PySpark batch job and identify partition, shuffle, cache, and join changes.

use_cases.title: Tune Cluster Memory Settings
use_cases.description: Choose executor memory, memory overhead, shuffle partitions, and storage settings for heavy workloads.

use_cases.title: Debug Skewed Analytics Queries
use_cases.description: Investigate uneven task duration and apply broadcast joins, salting, or AQE skew handling.

prompt_templates.title: Find Basic Bottlenecks
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of a slow Spark job
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this Spark job and list the most likely performance bottlenecks. Focus on partitions, shuffles, joins, caching, and file format choices.

prompt_templates.title: Tune Partitions and Shuffles
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate optimization for a known dataset size
prompt_templates.prompt: Given this dataset size, cluster shape, and Spark code, recommend partition counts, shuffle settings, and write layout changes. Explain each tradeoff.

prompt_templates.title: Fix Data Skew
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze these Spark UI metrics and query plan details. Identify skew, explain the likely cause, and propose AQE, broadcast, or salting changes.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Production Tuning Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a production Spark optimization plan for this pipeline. Include configuration changes, code changes, validation metrics, rollout steps, and rollback criteria.

output_examples.input: A nightly Spark aggregation spends most time in shuffle stages and writes many small files.
output: Primary diagnosis: excessive shuffle and poor output file sizing.
Recommended actions: enable AQE, right-size shuffle partitions, pre-aggregate before wide transformations, and adjust write partitioning.
Validation: compare shuffle read volume, spill metrics, output file counts, and total runtime before and after changes.

output_examples.input: A join between a large event table and a small dimension table is slow.
output: Primary diagnosis: the small table may not be broadcast, causing an avoidable shuffle.
Recommended actions: confirm dimension size, use a broadcast strategy when appropriate, and inspect the physical plan.
Validation: verify the join strategy in the plan and compare stage duration and shuffle volume.

output_examples.input: A Spark job fails intermittently with memory pressure during aggregation.
output: Primary diagnosis: aggregation may require too much execution memory or has skewed partitions.
Recommended actions: inspect spills, tune executor memory overhead, reduce partition size, and check skew ratio.
Validation: monitor spill bytes, garbage collection time, failed task count, and executor memory use.

best_practices: Start with Spark UI metrics and query plans before changing configuration.
Prefer built-in Spark SQL functions, columnar formats, and predicate pushdown over custom processing.
Validate every tuning change with runtime, shuffle, spill, file count, and cost metrics.
anti_patterns: Do not collect large datasets to the driver for convenience.
Do not cache every intermediate DataFrame without reuse and memory evidence.
Do not increase executor memory or partition counts without measuring the bottleneck.

faq.question: Does this skill run Spark jobs automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides optimization guidance and example patterns for users to adapt and run in their own environment.

faq.question: Can it help with PySpark and Spark SQL?
faq.answer: Yes. The guidance covers PySpark DataFrame patterns, Spark SQL query plans, and common runtime settings.

faq.question: What information should I provide for best results?
faq.answer: Provide code, dataset size, cluster shape, Spark version, query plan, Spark UI metrics, and failure symptoms.

faq.question: Does it support Databricks workloads?
faq.answer: Yes. Many patterns apply to Databricks, especially Delta Lake, AQE, file sizing, and query plan review.

faq.question: Will it always recommend more resources?
faq.answer: No. It emphasizes measurement, better partitioning, reduced shuffle, and efficient joins before larger clusters.

faq.question: Is this safe to publish in the marketplace?
faq.answer: Yes. Manual review found only Markdown guidance, benign sample code, and documentation links.
